name: HMS Kingston Jacinth (4.45)
events: 5 Jan 1942 At 1430 A/5, HMS P 38 (Lt. R.J. Hemingway, DSC, RN), departed Portsmouth for Gibraltar. She was escorted out by HMS Kingston Jacinth (Skr. A.G. Day, RNR) until 1730 A/5.

HMS P 38 was routed as follows; Q.Z.S. 111 and Q.Z.S. 169 (swept channels), 50°12'N, 01°18'W, 49°40'N. 05°00'W, 49°20'N, 11°00'W, 46°00'N, 13°00'W, Thence on the surface, due in position 43°00'N, 13°00'W at 1700 A/10 (In a signal timed 1651 A/7 from the Flag Officer Submarines (F.O.S.) this position and time were changed to 43°00'N, 11°30'W) at 0800 A/10. Further routing would be signalled by the Flag Officer Commanding North Atlantic (F.O.C.N.A.).
HMS P 38 was to proceed to Malta where she was to join the 10th Submarine Flotilla based there. (1)

7 Jun 1942 Around 1000 B/7 - 1130 B/7, HMS Torbay (Cdr. A.C.C. Miers, DSO and Bar, RN) and HMS Unbeaten (Lt.Cdr. E.A. Woodward, DSO, RN) made rendezvous in position 180° - Lizard Head - 10 miles, with their escort towards Portsmouth HMS Kingston Jacinth (Skr. A.G. Day, RNR). (2)
ADM numbers indicate documents at the British National Archives at Kew, London.
